title: Operator Summit: AI Workflows for Local Businesses
audience: Owners and operations leads at local service businesses
promise: A practical conference for implementing AI workflows without losing customer trust
description: Operator Summit brings service-business operators together for keynote sessions and panels and workshops and structured networking focused on measurable workflow gains.
accessibility: ADA accessible entrance with accessible seating and companion ticketing and elevator route and accessible restroom access
program: Registration and badge pickup at 8 AM; keynote session at 9 AM; customer support automation panel at 10:15 AM; sponsor booth demos at 11:30 AM; workshop tracks at 1:30 PM; closing session at 4:45 PM
ticketing: Early registration and standard registration and group tickets and sponsor badges
revenue_model: Registration revenue and sponsorship packages and exhibitor booths and qualified lead reports and group sales
cost_model: Venue and AV and speaker travel and sponsor booth buildout and registration staffing and security and insurance and catering and cleanup
break_even: Break-even at 275 registrations plus two silver sponsors
market_demand: Waitlist from prior operator breakfast series and partner email lists show demand from local business operators
sponsor_or_partner_value: Sponsors receive booth traffic and lead scans and session attribution and badge visibility and a post-event pipeline report
safety_plan: Security at registration and medical contact and crowd-flow signage and incident desk and emergency announcement plan and accessible evacuation route
marketing_plan: Partner newsletters and LinkedIn creator posts and chamber of commerce email and sponsor promotion and paid retargeting and repeat attendee capture
measurement_plan: Registration conversion and attendance and session satisfaction survey and NPS and sponsor leads and booth traffic and incident log and renewal intent

title: Riverfront R&B Sessions: Mara Vale Live
audience: Adults 21+ who follow independent R&B and Chicago nightlife
promise: An intimate live R&B bill with a headliner and opener and DJ
description: Mara Vale headlines a seated-then-standing R&B night at Riverfront Hall with an opener and DJ transitions and VIP balcony tickets and a post-show merch signing.
accessibility: ADA accessible entrance with accessible seating and companion ticketing and elevator route and accessible restroom access
program: Doors 8 PM; DJ set 8:30 PM; opener set 9:15 PM; headliner artist set 10:30 PM; merch signing 11:45 PM; close 1 AM
ticketing: General admission and VIP balcony and limited door tickets
revenue_model: Ticket sales and VIP balcony upgrades and merch and bar share and local beverage sponsor
cost_model: Artist guarantee and promoter fee and venue and production and security and medical and staffing and insurance and ticketing fees and cleanup
break_even: Break-even at 350 paid tickets after sponsor offset
market_demand: Comparable R&B events sold out 400-700 capacity rooms and artist socials and streaming data show local demand
sponsor_or_partner_value: Local beverage sponsor receives bar placement and creator content and post-event sales report
safety_plan: Security at entry and stage and medical contact and crowd-flow lanes and incident logging and rideshare zone and emergency exit briefing
marketing_plan: Artist socials and venue email and paid social and creator partners and retargeting and repeat-buyer capture
measurement_plan: Ticket sell-through and check-in rate and bar and merch revenue and incident rate and satisfaction survey and repeat-buyer capture

title: Prism Afterhours: Pride Weekend Circuit Party
audience: LGBTQ+ nightlife travelers and local circuit-party regulars 21+
promise: A high-production Pride weekend afterhours party with known DJs and hosts and performers
description: Prism Afterhours combines main-room dance sets and hosted balcony moments and guest performers and a dedicated decompression lounge for Pride weekend nightlife attendees.
accessibility: ADA accessible entrance with accessible viewing area and elevator route and accessible restrooms and companion ticket support
program: Doors 10 PM; host welcome 10:30 PM; DJ Aria main-room set 11 PM; performer showcase midnight; DJ Mateo afterhours set 1 AM; closing host set 3:30 AM
ticketing: Presale tickets and anytime entry and VIP table deposits and limited door tickets
revenue_model: Presales and door sales and VIP tables and bar share and sponsor placement and promoter list retention
cost_model: Venue rental and sound and lighting and talent and host fees and performer fees and security and medical and staffing and insurance and ticketing fees and cleanup
break_even: Break-even at 520 presale tickets or 430 presales plus six VIP tables
market_demand: Prior Pride weekend parties sold through VIP tables and the promoter list has strong repeat attendance
sponsor_or_partner_value: Hydration sponsor receives bar signage and hosted content and post-event attendance and safety recap
safety_plan: Security at entry and floor and medical room and crowd-flow lanes and incident reporting and harassment policy and rideshare zone and emergency egress briefing
marketing_plan: Promoter SMS list and DJ socials and host socials and LGBTQ+ media partners and creator content and paid retargeting and repeat-party capture
measurement_plan: Presale conversion and ticket check-in and bar revenue and incident safety reports and VIP table revenue and satisfaction pulse and repeat-buyer rate

title: The Glass Orchard: A New Chicago Play
audience: Theatergoers who follow new American plays and intimate ensemble productions
promise: A four-week run of a new family drama with named cast and director
description: The Glass Orchard is a two-act ensemble drama about inheritance and migration and a family farm with a local cast and talkbacks and group ticket blocks for theater clubs.
accessibility: ADA accessible entrance with wheelchair seating and companion ticketing and accessible restrooms and assisted-listening devices
program: Preview week begins Nov 5; Act I 7:30 PM; intermission 8:35 PM; Act II 8:50 PM; runtime 135 minutes; opening weekend talkback after Sunday matinee
ticketing: Preview tickets and standard tickets and student tickets and group sales and donor comps
revenue_model: Ticket sales and group sales and donor conversion and opening-night sponsor and subscription list growth
cost_model: Rights and royalty reserve and cast stipends and director fee and stage manager and venue and set and costumes and lighting and sound and marketing and insurance and front-of-house staffing
break_even: Break-even at 68 percent paid occupancy across the run
market_demand: Comparable blackbox dramas reached 70 percent occupancy and the theater email list supports new-play launches
sponsor_or_partner_value: Opening sponsor receives lobby credit and donor-night recognition and post-run audience report
safety_plan: Front-of-house security and medical contact and emergency exit briefing and incident log and accessible evacuation route and audience services desk
marketing_plan: Theater email list and cast interviews and playwright interviews and local arts press and student group outreach and donor preview and repeat audience capture
measurement_plan: Paid occupancy and ticket revenue and group sales and reviews and audience satisfaction survey and donor conversion and incident log and repeat attendance

title: Citywide Vocal Challenge: Finals Night
audience: Singers 16+ and fans of live talent competitions
promise: A live finals event with audition-qualified contestants and judges and audience voting
description: Citywide Vocal Challenge is a finals-night talent competition for audition-qualified singers with judge scoring and audience voting and elimination rounds and a winner package.
age_policy: All ages with guardian required for minors
accessibility: ADA accessible entrance with accessible seating and companion ticketing and elevator route and accessible restroom access
program: Doors 6 PM; host welcome 6:45 PM; contestant round one 7 PM; judge feedback 8 PM; audience vote opens 8:20 PM; elimination 8:45 PM; final round 9 PM; winner announced 9:45 PM
ticketing: Contestant guest blocks and general admission tickets and sponsor seats and livestream passes
revenue_model: Applications and ticketed finals and livestream passes and sponsor package and audience vote engagement and content clips
cost_model: Venue and production and judges and host and contestant support and prize studio time and voting platform and livestream crew and security and medical and insurance and marketing
break_even: Break-even at 430 tickets plus one prize sponsor
market_demand: Audition applications exceeded finalist slots and prior vocal showcases retained strong audience engagement
sponsor_or_partner_value: Sponsor receives prize naming and livestream mentions and content clips and winner follow-up report
safety_plan: Security at entry and stage and medical contact and contestant check-in and crowd-flow aisles and incident reporting and youth guardian protocol and emergency communication
marketing_plan: Contestant social toolkits and judge videos and sponsor channels and local music schools and paid social and livestream clips and audience retention email
measurement_plan: Application count and audition completion and ticket sales and livestream viewers and vote totals and audience retention and engagement and incident log and winner outcomes and satisfaction survey

title: Lakefront Summer Arts Festival
audience: Families and art buyers and music fans and weekend visitors looking for a walkable local festival
promise: A two-day lakefront festival with music lineup and artist vendors and food and local economic impact
description: Lakefront Summer Arts Festival fills the park with two stages and a curated artist vendor market and food trucks and family workshops and evening music sets.
accessibility: ADA accessible routes with accessible viewing zones and accessible portable restrooms and companion seating areas and service animal policy
program: Gates 11 AM; maker market noon; family workshop 1 PM; acoustic stage lineup 2 PM; main stage artist sets 5 PM; host announcements between sets; food vendor row open all day; site map at entries
ticketing: Weekend passes and single-day tickets and family packs and sponsor guest passes and vendor credentials
revenue_model: Ticket sales and sponsor packages and vendor fees and concessions and merch and parking partner share and grants and tourism partnership
cost_model: Park permit and stages and sound and lighting and talent and vendor operations and security and medical and accessible restrooms and sanitation and waste cleanup and insurance and marketing
break_even: Break-even at 2800 paid attendees plus 65 vendor booths and two sponsors
market_demand: Prior park events drew strong attendance and tourism partners want a repeatable summer weekend draw
sponsor_or_partner_value: Sponsors receive stage naming and vendor-lane signage and content and attendance report and local economic impact recap
safety_plan: Security at gates and stages and medical tent and crowd-flow lanes and weather monitoring and incident command and emergency announcements and accessible evacuation routes and cleanup plan
marketing_plan: Artist lineup announcements and vendor spotlights and tourism partner email and local press and creator content and paid social and repeat attendee capture
measurement_plan: Attendance and dwell time and vendor sales and per-cap spend and sponsor impressions and incident rate and weather impacts and satisfaction survey and repeat intent
